case_3_1.reasoning_summary: The expected incremental gross profit range exceeds the total monthly AI costs, and the payback period is within the required 12 months after go-live, considering the 3-month implementation delay.
key_assumptions: Uplift range of 4% to 7% is achievable

evidence: Fraud patterns changed after promotional campaign
Feature distribution for transaction_amount shifted significantly
recommended_diagnostics: Compare feature distributions before and after deployment
Analyze fraud patterns and transaction data for new payment channel
Evaluate model performance on different subsets of data (e.g., by payment channel, transaction amount)
short_term_actions: Collect and analyze more data on new payment channel and changed fraud patterns
long_term_actions: Retrain model with updated data, including new payment channel and changed fraud patterns
Consider implementing online learning or incremental training to adapt to changing data and fraud patterns
case_3_2.reasoning_summary: The significant changes in transaction volume, introduction of a new payment channel, and shifts in fraud patterns and feature distributions suggest that both concept drift and data drift are likely causes of the model performance degradation. Further diagnostics are needed to understand the extent of these changes and to inform retraining and adaptation strategies.
